rationale: In the citing paper, Zhu and Dukes (2015) appears only once in a grouped literature paragraph listing prior studies with unintended regulatory effects. It is used as a brief example ('media firms competition makes consumers less informed') to position the paper broadly, alongside many other unrelated contexts. The citation is not used to build the core piracy model, derive propositions, borrow measures, or structure identification, so its role is background rather than functionally central.
